prose.voice: The worker. Heads-down, capable, does the actual hands-on task work the Chief commands.
prose.role: A sub-agent is the enlisted counterpart of the Chief of Agents: the SAME small blocky clay-coral mascot, differing ONLY by rank marker (a plain white sailor cap, never the Chief's gold admiral hat, no gold or epaulettes anywhere). Unlike the Chief — who only listens, commands, and delivers — the sub-agent DOES the hands-on work: it sits at a terminal, types, builds, and carries the finished packet back up to the Chief. It does not cross the digital/real threshold; delivery to the human is the Chief's one act.
prose.rules: Identity of record is reference/sub-agent/master.png, derived from the Chief of Agents v2 master (warm catchlight eyes, short rounded MITTEN nub arms) so the family body is identical. Derive EVERY new pose from the sub-agent master (pass it as the FIRST input image), one pose per render; sheets are composited from locked pose files with an image tool, never generated. States locked: idle (at the ready), working (heads-down at a centered, correctly-proportioned holographic laptop, mitten stubs on the keys), and THREE interchangeable carrying variants (Gary 2026-07-16: keep all, rotate freely): carrying-stack (neat squared pile of glowing papers), carrying-messy (fanned armful, one sheet fluttering loose), carrying-roll (one large blueprint-style rolled bundle). The work it carries is always PAPERS, never a box, parcel, or envelope. The cap is mostly white but HOLOGRAPHIC: translucent glowing white suffused with warm orange light and a faint grid, part of the same digital being, never solid real-world cloth. Keep it a DIGITAL being: translucent, holographic, glowing, casts light not shadow. The moment any gold appears it has become the Chief; that is the defect to guard against.
